title: EdgeClear Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of EdgeClear pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
EdgeClear Pricing Overview

EdgeClear is a Chicago-based, CFTC-registered futures brokerage that positions itself as a commission-conscious alternative for active traders. The platform operates on a tiered pricing model with two core offerings: a free standard account and a premium tier adding Rithmic data and API access for $20/month. The headline feature is micro futures commissions starting at just $0.20 per contract—among the lowest in the industry—making EdgeClear particularly attractive for day traders executing high-volume strategies.

However, EdgeClear's published pricing is intentionally sparse. While the company advertises low commissions and transparent fee structures, the minimum account deposit, exact commissions across all contract types, and nuanced fee schedules are not publicly disclosed. New traders will need to contact EdgeClear directly for a complete pricing breakdown, which reflects the firm's white-glove approach of assigning every account a dedicated personal broker.

All EdgeClear Plans Compared

EdgeClear offers two primary tiers, both significantly simpler than competitors offering dozens of plan variations:

Feature Standard Account Rithmic Data + API
Monthly Cost $0/mo $20/mo
Micro futures commissions From $0.20/contract From $0.20/contract
Dedicated personal broker ✓ Included ✓ Included
EdgeWatch analytics ✓ Included ✓ Included
Platform access (TradingView, CQG, Rithmic, MotiveWave, TT) ✓ All platforms ✓ All platforms
Rithmic direct data connection ✗ ✓ Included
API access for algo trading ✗ ✓ Included
Global exchange access (CME, Eurex, ICE, CBOE, Asian) ✓ ✓

Standard Account ($0/mo): EdgeClear's entry tier removes barriers to access by eliminating subscription fees entirely. You get the core brokerage experience: micro futures trading with commissions from $0.20/contract, a dedicated personal broker assigned to your account, complimentary trade performance analytics through EdgeWatch, and access to multiple trading platforms. The Standard Account is ideal for traders who prefer building custom integrations through existing third-party platforms or who don't require direct Rithmic API connectivity.

Rithmic Data + API ($20/mo): The premium tier ($240 annually) adds two significant capabilities. Direct Rithmic data connection provides ultra-low-latency market data feeds critical for high-frequency strategies, while API access unlocks automated and algorithmic trading. For developers and traders deploying bots, this tier is the practical requirement. At $20/month, the upgrade cost is negligible for any trader executing 200+ contracts monthly (the $20 fee would be recouped in 100 micro contracts at standard pricing).

Free Plan / Free Trial

EdgeClear does not advertise a free trial period. However, the Standard Account tier itself is free—there are no monthly or platform fees to maintain an account. The only costs are transaction-based: commissions on trades and potential exchange fees.

This is a critical distinction. While you won't pay to open or maintain the account, actual trading will incur costs immediately. Edge Clear's minimum account deposit is not publicly listed, so prospective traders should contact the firm directly to confirm the minimum required to trade. This is a deliberate design choice—EdgeClear reserves the right to scale minimum deposits based on account type and trading intent, which may range from $5,000 to $25,000 depending on regulatory requirements and broker discretion.

Is the free account worth starting with? Yes, if you can meet the minimum deposit. You get the full trading experience with personalized broker support and analytics—there's no feature lockdown designed to upsell you. The decision to upgrade to Rithmic + API is purely about technical capability, not because the free tier is artificially limited.

Hidden Costs and Fees

EdgeClear's transparency about base pricing contrasts sharply with opacity around total cost of ownership. Several cost categories exist beyond the headline figures:

  • Exchange and clearinghouse fees: Futures contracts incur fees levied by exchanges (CME, CBOE, etc.) and clearinghouses. These vary by contract and are typically $0.50–$2.00 per contract on top of EdgeClear's commissions. EdgeClear does not publicly itemize these costs.
  • Data feeds and market data: The Standard Account does not include direct Rithmic connectivity. If you use platforms like TradingView or CQG through EdgeClear's integration, you may incur third-party data feed costs depending on your subscription tier with those platforms. This is not an EdgeClear fee per se, but a hidden ecosystem cost.
  • API and infrastructure costs: While Rithmic API access is included at $20/mo, deploying algorithms at scale may require additional compute resources, hosting, or infrastructure—costs borne by the trader, not EdgeClear.
  • Minimum deposit and account minimums: EdgeClear does not publicly disclose these figures. Contact the firm for exact requirements, which may be higher than competitors.
  • Inactivity and account maintenance: EdgeClear's published materials do not mention inactivity fees, but this should be confirmed directly with your broker.

The lack of transparent fee schedules is EdgeClear's largest pricing weakness. Traders accustomed to Interactive Brokers' itemized fee displays will find EdgeClear's model frustrating. However, the firm's rationale is straightforward: every account includes a dedicated personal broker who can explain fees in context of your specific trading strategy. This white-glove approach prioritizes customization over transparency—a trade-off that works well for serious traders but may frustrate retail experimenters.

EdgeClear Pricing vs Competitors

EdgeClear's micro futures commission structure ($0.20 per contract minimum) is genuinely competitive. Here's how it stacks up against well-known alternatives:

Broker Micro Futures Commission* Account Fee Dedicated Support
EdgeClear $0.20/contract $0 (free) or $20/mo ✓ Yes (every account)
Interactive Brokers $0.10–$0.15/contract** $0 (or $10/mo waived if active) ✗ Limited
Futures.io (NinjaTrader) $0.50–$1.00/contract** $0 ✓ Community-based
Lightspeed Futures $0.50–$1.00/contract** $0 ✗ Minimal

*Prices listed are approximate based on public information and exclude exchange/clearinghouse fees. Contact brokers directly for current rates.

Key insights:

  • Interactive Brokers edges out EdgeClear on pure commission cost ($0.10–$0.15 vs $0.20/contract), but IB's interface is notoriously complex and support is minimal unless you pay for premium. EdgeClear's guaranteed personal broker adds significant value that IB charges extra for (or doesn't offer).
  • EdgeClear beats traditional futures brokers decisively. Firms like Futures.io and Lightspeed charge 2–5x more per contract while offering no dedicated support. EdgeClear's $0.20 commission plus a dedicated broker is genuinely differentiated.
  • Hidden costs matter. Interactive Brokers has a $10/month account fee (waived if you're active enough). EdgeClear has $0 or $20/mo depending on tier. Over a year, this compounds: IB = $0–$120, EdgeClear = $0–$240. For traders executing 200+ contracts/month, EdgeClear's per-contract advantage erases this fee gap.

Is EdgeClear Worth the Price?

For active micro futures traders (200+ contracts/month): EdgeClear is excellent value. The $0.20/contract commission is competitive, and the included personal broker justifies the platform choice alone. Upgrade to Rithmic + API ($20/mo) only if you need algorithmic trading or ultra-low-latency data.

For casual traders or experimenters: EdgeClear's minimum deposit (unspecified, likely $5k–$25k) and lack of a true free trial are friction points. If you're testing strategies, Interactive Brokers or a micro-brokerage might be better entry points.

For algorithmic traders: The Rithmic + API tier ($20/mo) is a no-brainer. API access plus dedicated support is a rare combination; most brokers offer one or the other, not both.

For global traders: EdgeClear's broad exchange access (CME, Eurex, ICE, CBOE, Asian exchanges) justifies the platform if you trade outside US markets. Competitors often have higher minimums or worse execution for non-US contracts.

How to Save on EdgeClear

EdgeClear's pricing structure leaves little room for discounts or optimization:

  • No promotional codes or coupons: EdgeClear does not publicly advertise discounts. Ask your assigned personal broker if volume-based rebates or promotional commission reductions are available for larger accounts.
  • Volume rebates: High-volume traders (1000+ contracts/month) should inquire about tiered commission structures. Many brokers privately negotiate rates for serious traders.
  • Annual billing: The Rithmic + API tier is charged monthly ($20/mo = $240/year). No annual discount is advertised, but this is worth negotiating with your broker.
  • Offset data costs: If you already subscribe to CQG or TradingView premium, EdgeClear's Standard Account avoids redundant data fees. Using EdgeClear's free tier + existing platform subscriptions is often cheaper than competitors' bundles.
  • Maximize the free tier: EdgeClear's Standard Account ($0/mo) includes EdgeWatch analytics and personal broker support—features that cost extra elsewhere. Avoid paying for Rithmic + API unless you genuinely need API access or direct Rithmic latency.

Conclusion: EdgeClear offers genuinely competitive pricing for active futures traders, particularly those valuing personal support and micro-contract execution. The catch: minimum deposits and full fee schedules are not public, requiring a direct conversation with the firm. For traders ready to commit capital and execute volume, EdgeClear's combination of low commissions and dedicated brokerage is hard to beat. For experimenters, the friction is higher than competitors.

Ready to explore EdgeClear further? Read the full EdgeClear review on TradingToolsHub or compare EdgeClear to other futures brokers.
